author: Coursicle
text:
ISE 300 at SBU - Reviews & Ratings, Fall 2026 professors

Professor Reviews

Make sure to do everything she asks. Look at the specifications for each assignment. One big writing assignment and the rest should be fairly simple and a presentation. Takes attendance randomly. Tells you everything she requires and expects, so no surprises. She's very considerate.

This course should be an automatic A for anyone taking it with professor Liu. She takes more time than any professor I've had to make it EXTREMELY clear exactly what topics will be covered, what she expects on papers, and what to do on presentations. She actually cares about students and makes a concerted effort to help in any and every way. Liu >

Description

Principles of professional technical communications for Computer Science and Information Systems majors. Topics include writing business communications, user manuals, press releases, literature reviews, and research abstracts. Persuasive oral communications and effective presentation techniques, to address a range of audiences, will also be covered. This course satisfies the upper-division writing requirement for CSE and ISE majors. Class Notes: Offered as ISE 300 and CSE 300. Enrollment Requirements: Prerequisites: WRT 102; CSE or ISE or DAS major; U3 or U4 standing.

Usually Offered

TuTh (1 hour 20 minutes), MW (1 hour 20 minutes)

Attributes

SPK Speak Effectively before an Audience WRTD Write Effectively within One's Discipline
